author: potuz
content: I don't think the reason was complexity as Toni put it. The real reason was that it makes harder for single binary clients to sync in the most efficient way. What they hope for is that there is a single P2P network that downloads all the payloads and they hope that the removed one is the one on the EL. I believe this was a big mistake in many levels, but anyway, we already have syncing implemented. Hopefully Lean comes soon enough and we can ditch the entire CL codebase that's becoming harder to maintain.

author: taulepton_
content: Thank you for the explanation. execution proofs may provide a beacon of hope here. With execution proofs, chain proofs and EIP8237 we can sync without downloading any payloads or historical proofs at all. With a single chain proof (recursive execution proof), the node can verify the execution chain from the checkpoint to the finalised block. This saves a lot of bandwidth and is much faster.  If this EIP doesn't land in gloas then I think execution proofs may provide motivation for reconsideration in the future. cc: <@427491045308235776>

author: taulepton_
content: The alternative is that the execution proof node has to fetch and verify execution proofs for every block from the checkpoint to the finalised block, which is wasteful.

author: potuz
content: problem is that the signal here was that the CL will always need to download payloads to drive the EL, regardless of proofs or not, you may sync only with blocks, but you still need to download to serve payloads by range! what I wanted to avoid is this burden on the CL.

author: taulepton_
content: In future upgrades, nodes should be able to advertise (lack of) support for the payloads by range method. execution proof nodes don't advertise the capability, and full EL nodes do.

author: potuz
content: from the user point of view this is fine: if you want to run a zk client you do not download the full data, but the point I wanted to avoid is maintaining this code complexity of serving and downloading on the CL. Now the CL node will have to keep this, regardless if the user decides to never use it

author: potuz
content: when the CL should not have had to download them at all in the first place

author: taulepton_
content: I see. I had only considered the impact on physical resources.
